search_session.schema_note: Adapted from shared search-session schema by setting family='haqiqi_witness_census'. Per-portal search data captured in searches_completed[]; new witnesses (zero in this run) and supplementary data captured below.

searches_completed.notes: Person record links 3 BL manuscripts: Or 9493 (manuscript_6481, GENUINE), Or 5327 (manuscript_5950, FP: contains Nuzhat al-Mulūk by Muḥammad al-Bulghārī, only mentioned in passing as being 'written in the reign of Jahānshāh Qaraqoyunlu'), Or 11846 (manuscript_5394, FP: Hāfiẓ Sa'd dīvān copied for Jahānshāh's son Pīr Būdāq in 864 AH; commissioner relationship, NOT authorship by Jahānshāh). Person record also lists work_8619 'Nuzhat al-Mulūk' as 'authored or contributed to' but this is incorrect: that work is by al-Bulghārī, not Jahānshāh.
Work record lists exactly one witness: Or 9493 (manuscript_6481), 15th c. CE. No other Fihrist-indexed witnesses for this work.

Hits: Or 9493 (genuine); RAS Ellis Persian 12 (Tabaqāt-i Akbarī by Niẓām al-Dīn Aḥmad: FP); CODRINGTON/READE NO 59 BOX 28 (same Tabaqāt-i Akbarī: FP); Or 16420 (Persian translation of Chaghnini astronomy treatise: FP, ḥaqīqī as adjective); IO Islamic 4055 (18th c. Indian miscellany of prose and poetry: FP, no Hakîkî mention); RSPA 14 (collection of scientific treatises: FP); plus the person record. Person-record name disambiguation: every modern indexer attaches multiple ḥaqīqī-named works to Cihanşah's authority record, but only Or 9493 is actually his.

11 hits: all FPs except Or 9493. The remaining 10 use ḥaqīqī as the Sufi/philosophical adjective 'real, true' (in opposition to majāzī 'metaphorical') in mystical, philosophical, astronomical, or epistolary contexts. Notable FPs: SOAS ms. 24950 (Tabaqāt-i Akbar Shāhī); Wellcome MS Arabic 507 (Sharḥ-i ḥaz̤rat dar ʿilm-i nujūm by Naṣīr al-Dīn Ṭūsī); BL IO Islamic 4570 (mystical tracts); Wellcome MS Arabic 286 (Risālah fī ādāb al-baḥth); Cambridge UL L. 1 (52 letters by Rashīd al-Dīn); BL RSPA 14 (scientific treatises); JRRIL Persian MS 735 (Bāznāmah falconry); JRRIL Persian MS 961 (Sānval Singh translation); Wellcome MS Arabic 291 (al-Rusūm al-falsafīyah). Note: 4 of 10 codices have decoration recorded in Fihrist.
Zero results: Fihrist cataloguing exclusively uses 'Haqiqi' transliteration (IJMES), not Turkish 'Hakiki'.
Zero results.
Zero results across all Turkish-orthography variants. Fihrist does not index the Turkish-orthography form of the name.
5 hits: person record + work record + Or 9493 + Or 11846 + Or 5327. Same 3 BL codices documented above plus the person/work index entries.

Far too broad: matches Mughal Shah Jahan, Khan Jahan Lodi (d. 1631), and many other unrelated 'Shah' rulers. Only Or 9493 is the genuine Cihanşah Hakîkî witness; not feasible to triage 1703 FPs for this scope.
2 hits: person record + Or 11846 (Hāfiẓ Sa'd dīvān copied for Pīr Būdāq, FP commissioner relation). Or 9493 not returned for this term: Fihrist uses 'Qaraqoyunlu' spelling exclusively.
Same as Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu phrase search.
Returns multiple hits but only Or 9493 is the Cihanşah Hakîkî Dīvān; all others are matches in Mughal/Persian historical contexts (Shāh-Jahān etc.).

Single hit: Supplément turc 1497 (Divân-i Hakîkî). Per the BnF authority field 'Hakikizâde, Osman Efendi (....-1627)' this is the SON of a different Hakîkî (Hakîkî-zâde = 'son of Hakîkî'), Ottoman Bektaşi/Melamî poet, distinct from Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu Hakîkî (d. 1467). Persistent ID ./ark:/12148/cc91114j.

Hits: (1) Persan 39, sub-component F.92v-94, ŠARḤ-i MUŠKIL-i ḤALL by Hamadānī (Mīr Sayyid ʿAlī b. Šihāb al-Dīn): FP, the explicit contains 'مطلوب حقیقی' as Sufi adjective. (2) Supplément persan 114 'Risāla fī bayān-i ʿishq-i majāzī va ḥaqīqī' (Treatise on real and metaphorical love): FP, ḥaqīqī as Sufi technical term.

searches_completed.search_term: Cihansah / Cihanshah / Karakoyunlu / Kara-Koyounlou / Dschihan / Hakyky / Cahansah / Cahanşah / حقیقی، جهانشاه / جهانشاه قراقویونلو / جهانشاه (alone)
searches_completed.notes: All zero results. BnF Archives & Manuscrits indexes do not contain Cihanşah Hakîkî (or any of his name-variant spellings).

Djihan Chah=1 result (Smith-Lesouëf 246 'Recueil des portraits des empereurs moghols': FP, Mughal Shah Jahan). Djahan=3 results (Boulez correspondence, Arabe 4529 Taj Mahal Quranic inscriptions, Smith-Lesouëf 247 calligraphy collection: all FP).

Hits: (1) Supplément turc 1455 'Ganîzâde Mehmed Nâdirî. Davhatü'l-inşâ': different Ottoman poet, FP. (2-3) Two copies (digitization + microfilm) of Turc 270 'Recueil de divans de poètes turcs': match is 'Hakki' (= Ismaʿil Hakki, d. 1725, NOT Cihanşah-Hakîkî). FP.

Inspected full Blochet description of Turc 270 (322 ff., 17th c., XVIIe siècle Turkish nestalik, Ottoman binding). Poets in main page block: Zati, Fighani, Sanʿi, Shemʿi, Receb Dede ʿAdeni, Hilali, Behishti, Oumidi Roumi Ahmed Çelebi, ʿAmravi, Mesihi, Ǧelili, Baki Çelebi, Enveri, Khayali, Soubouhi. Marginal poets: Sultan Bayezid II ʿAdli, Safi, Vaǧhi, Katibi, Tâliʿi, Mesihi (different), Hakki = Ismaʿil Hakki, Âhi (d. 1517), Amri Çelebi (d. 1580). NO Cihanşah Hakîkî in this anthology. The 'Hakki' is the 17th-c. Bursalı Ismaʿil Hakki: different person.

searches_completed.search_term: Hakiki / Cihanshah Hakiki / Karakoyunlu / Qara Qoyunlu / Djihan Chah / Djehan Schah / Cihansah / Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu / Jahan Shah Qaraqoyunlu
searches_completed.notes: BnF Catalogue général (printed books + AV): zero codex hits. 'Hakiki' returns 2 modern academic hits (a 2009 medical physics textbook by Nour Eddine Hakiki; a 1988 UNESCO youth-employment study with Fatiha Hakiki-Talahite contributing). Karakoyunlu/Qara Qoyunlu both return Sandra Aube's 2016 PUPS book on Qaraqoyunlu/Aq Qoyunlu architectural ceramics (BnF cote 738.095 AUBE c). All other variants zero.
1 hit: Supplément turc 1497 (Hakîkî zâde Osman Efendi): same FP as BnF AeM.

searches_completed.search_term: Cihanshah / Jahanshah / Qaraqoyunlu / Karakoyunlu / Djehan Schah
searches_completed.notes: All zero results. CCFR covers all French manuscript collections (BnF, Calames academic libraries, CGM municipal manuscripts, BnF-AM departmental archives). No Cihanşah Hakîkî codex anywhere in France.
Smith-Lesouëf 246 (Mughal portraits collection): same FP.

9 manuscripts found across 6 European institutions. KEY HIT IS A DISAMBIGUATION FALSE POSITIVE: DE-SBB Ms. or. oct. 2746 - 1 'İrşâdnâme by Ḥaḳîḳî' is by a HOMONYMOUS 17TH-CENTURY HAKÎKÎ (d. 1050/1640-41), a Melamî dervish per Gölpinarli 1931, *Melâmîlik ve Melâmîler*, Istanbul, p. 211: NOT Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu. Qalamos person record https://www.qalamos.net/receive/MyMssPerson_agent_00014071 explicitly gives death date 1050/1640-41. Other 8 hits are: CH-BASUB UBH M VI 31 (anonymous Ottoman collective ms 1838); DE-SLUB Mscr.Dresd.Ea.124 (Tesnîm by Bābā Sinān); DE-TUEB Ma VII 104 (Kitâb-ı Cebbâr Kulu, anonymous, 1284/1868): multiple records for same shelfmark; ID-EHI Crb/EH/31/2012 (Javanese-Arabic miscellany); 2x ID-MUSALI Yemen records. NONE relate to Cihanşah Hakîkî.
All zero results across these variants.
DE-SBB Hs. or. 1063 'Künhü'l-aḫbâr by ʿÂlî, Muṣṭafâ bin Aḥmed': Ottoman historian's universal history mentioning Qaraqoyunlu in passing. FP.

Search interface displays '0 of 234125' across all collections. The portal requires login for record-level inspection but the 0-match facet count confirms no Hakiki-attributed records in their manuscript bibliographic index. Note the index is largely Arabic and African collections (Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, Bosnia, Saudi Arabia, Egyptian regional libraries) with limited Persian/Turkish poetry coverage.

totals.false_positives_total: 30+ unique false positives across all portals (excluding tally-only hit counts)
totals.duplicate_note: Or 9493 = bl_or_9493 in witness_register.json: already verified, not a new finding.

supplementary_data_for_existing_witnesse.value: Title: Dīvān / دیوان; Author: Jahānshāh Qaraqoyunlu Ḥaqīqī (جهانشاه قره قویونلی حقیقی); Date: 893 AH (1488 CE); Languages: 'Persian and Turkish'; Folios: '85 ff'; Dimensions: '23 × 16 cm' (leaf); Form: Codex; Contents per Fihrist: 'Contains poems in Persian 1r-45v as well as Turkish'.
supplementary_data_for_existing_witnesse.notes: Confirms register data exactly. CRITICAL NEGATIVE FINDING: Fihrist record contains NO decoration/illumination/binding/paper/scribe data fields populated. The 'Decoration' facet on the Fihrist search page tags Or 9493 as 'No' (i.e., no decoration recorded). This means: (a) Meredith-Owens 1968 *Handlist of Persian Manuscripts* p. 57: the cited bibliography: likely did not record decoration either; (b) the BL has not yet uploaded extended metadata for this codex; (c) for the decoration question to be answered, direct examination or new high-resolution imaging is required. This is the strongest open-access evidence yet that Or 9493 has minimal-to-no significant illumination: but could still have decorated paper that wouldn't be captured under 'illumination' or by Meredith-Owens' brief listing.

The Fihrist search facet 'Decoration: No 2 / Yes 1' over the 3 codices in the Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu phrase search: and the corresponding facet 'Decoration: No' on Or 9493's individual record: indicates Or 9493 was catalogued WITHOUT decoration-positive metadata. The 'Yes' codex is Or 11846 (Pīr Būdāq Hāfiẓ dīvān, with 1 miniature). Caveat: Fihrist 'Decoration No' is a coarse cataloguer judgment derived from Meredith-Owens; absence of recorded decoration ≠ absence of decoration. Decorated paper (sprinkled gold, marbled, halkâr, etc.) would not necessarily register as 'decoration' in this scheme.

supplementary_data_for_existing_witnesse.value: Author authority: VIAF 43402797, LoC n88215419, Fihrist person_43402797. Work authority: Fihrist work_20697.
supplementary_data_for_existing_witnesse.notes: Confirms search_keys.json external_identifiers section. These are the only Fihrist-indexed manuscripts attributed to Jahānshāh Qaraqoyunlu's authority record.

rejected_candidates.rejection_reason: FALSE POSITIVE. Or 5327 contains 5 works including 'Nuzhat al-Mulūk' by Muḥammad al-Bulghārī (ff. 215v-263r), described as 'written in the reign of Jahānshāh Qaraqoyunlu': this is a HISTORICAL CONTEXT note, not authorship. Jahānshāh did not author the Nuzhat. The Fihrist person_43402797 record incorrectly lists Or 5327 as a 'manuscript' authored by Jahānshāh, which is metadata drift. 17-18th c. CE, 296 ff., 26.3 × 16.4 cm, all 5 works in Persian.

FALSE POSITIVE for Haqiqi authorship. Or 11846 is a Dīvān by Hāfiẓ Sa'd (حافظ سعد), 864 AH (1460), 185 ff., 25.7 × 13 cm, COPIED FOR THE LIBRARY of Pīr Būdāq, son of Jahānshāh Qaraqoyunlu (commissioner relationship). Decoration: '1 miniature' on f. 148r depicting 'Pīr Būdāḳ, son of Jahānshāh Ḳarāḳoyunlu, reclining in a garden listening to a lute player'. NOT a witness to Haqiqi poetry, but VALUABLE AS COMPARATOR for contemporary Qaraqoyunlu codicology: copied 7 years before Jahānshāh's death (d. 1467) by a different scribe in his son's atelier.
rejected_candidates.digital_surrogate_note: Available via BL Digitised Manuscripts at http://www.bl.uk/manuscripts/FullDisplay.aspx?ref=Or_11846; reader's card required for access.

rejected_candidates.rejection_reason: FALSE POSITIVE: DISAMBIGUATION CASE. Qalamos lists this as 'İrşâdnâme by Ḥaḳîḳî' (Ottoman-Turkish, text from collective manuscript). However the Qalamos agent record at https://www.qalamos.net/receive/MyMssPerson_agent_00014071 explicitly identifies this Ḥaḳîḳî as the Melamî dervish who DIED 1050/1640-41: citing Gölpinarli 1931, *Melâmîlik ve Melâmîler*, Istanbul, p. 211. This is a DIFFERENT POET from Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu Hakîkî (d. 872/1467). Earlier 4-way ambiguity (later expanded to 7-way in the final register) and TEIS Yesevi caveat. unresolved question for future researcher: should this 17th-c. Hakîkî be added to a SEPARATE register of homonymous Hakîkîs to prevent future re-discovery? It's the second known Hakîkî (after the Hakîkîzâde Osman Efendi at BnF Supplément turc 1497).

FALSE POSITIVE: DISAMBIGUATION CASE. BnF authority field gives 'Hakikizâde, Osman Efendi (....-1627)': i.e., 'son of Hakîkî, Osman Efendi'. The pen-name suffix '-zâde' (son of) plus the 1627 death date confirm this is a different Ottoman Bektaşi/Melamî 17th-c. poet, not Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 1467). ARK ./ark:/12148/cc91114j. Same homonymy pattern as the Berlin Hakîkî above; possibly the same Melamî 'family' tradition (-zâde would mean Osman Efendi was son of an earlier Hakîkî).

false_positive_patterns: Pen-name homonymy [later expanded to 7-way disambiguation]: AT LEAST FOUR distinct 'Hakîkî' poets were catalogued across European holdings during this UK/European sweep: (1) Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu, d. 1467 (Or 9493 only: the target); (2) Hakîkîzâde Osman Efendi, d. 1627 (BnF Suppl. turc 1497); (3) the Melamî Ḥaḳîḳî, d. 1050/1640-41 (Berlin Ms. or. oct. 2746/1, İrşâdnâme; per Gölpinarli 1931); (4) probable others flagged in witness_register.json (Ay 2013 caveat) but not yet encountered in this UK/European scope. Any 'Hakîkî' identification with a date later than ~1500 should default to disambiguation pending; only Cihanşah is a Qaraqoyunlu witness.

Sufi adjective ḥaqīqī (real, true) vs majāzī (metaphorical): Persian-script searches return ~10 false positives where ḥaqīqī appears as a philosophical adjective in mystical, philosophical, astronomical, or epistolary contexts. These are NEVER witness-authorial usages. Disambiguate by checking whether the term occurs in colophon, title-page, or attributive byline context (= author) vs. body of text (= adjective).

Mughal Shah Jahan / Jihān-shāh confusion: 19th-c. Orientalist transliterations 'Djihan Chah', 'Djihân Châh', 'Schah Djahan' typically refer to the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan (r. 1628-58, builder of Taj Mahal), NOT Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu. Smith-Lesouëf 246 (Mughal portraits) and Arabe 4529 (Taj Mahal Quran inscriptions) are diagnostic FPs.

Commissioner / dedicatee / historical-context conflations: BL Or 5327 (Nuzhat al-Mulūk 'written in the reign of Jahānshāh') and Or 11846 (Hāfiẓ dīvān 'copied for the library of Pīr Būdāq, son of Jahānshāh') are indexed under person_43402797 in Fihrist via their HISTORICAL relationship, not authorship. Distinguish: authorial witness vs. commissioner witness vs. contemporary-period witness: only the first qualifies for the Haqiqi register.

Ottoman 'Hakki' (Ismaʿil Hakki, d. 1725) is a distinct poet whose name is sometimes orthographically confused with 'Hakîkî' in older transliterations. BnF Turc 270 marginal divan section was indexed under 'Hakki' but is the 17th-18th c. Bursalı Ismaʿil Hakki: not a Hakîkî homonym.

blocked_or_inaccessible_portals.reason: CloudFront 403 from this network. Search could not be completed through automated retrieval. Cambridge holdings are however well covered by Fihrist (Trinity College, Christ's, Newton, etc.).

Login required for record-level inspection. Facet count of '0 of 234125' is observable without login and indicates no Hakiki-attributed records, but cannot verify by examining individual records. Note Al-Furqan's index is heavily Arabic/African collection-focused with limited Persian/Turkish poetry coverage anyway.
